title: Singapore design value: higher perceived value, better compensation fairness
summary: Singapore designers report stronger value recognition: 73.3% feel valued (ratings 5-7, vs 70.2% regional). Fair compensation: 72.2% feel fairly compensated (vs 51.4% regional). User-centricity: 63.1% report strong user prioritisation (vs 57.7%). Design advocacy: 70.6% report education efforts, with 39.1% high effort (vs regional higher). The data suggests more mature design integration in Singapore organizations.
methodology: Self-reported perception of design value, compensation fairness, user-centricity, and advocacy efforts in anonymous online survey, filtered to Singapore
